name: Decode protobuf bytes into a JavaScript object
info:
The function node supplies a hard-coded protobuf message in hex form - this is exactly what protobuf wire bytes look like. They were produced by the encode node (any protobuf library produces the same bytes) from this payload:

    {"timestamp":1533295590569,"foo":1.5,"bar":true,"test":"Hello from the decode example","noMoreSnakeCase":true}

Click the inject button: the decode node turns the Buffer back into that JavaScript object using the `TestType` message from the proto file.

Tip: setting `msg.protobufType` on an incoming message overrides the type configured in the node.

The protobuf-file config node points at:

    node_modules/@pauldeng/node-red-contrib-protobuf/examples/protos/proto3.proto

This relative path works when Node-RED runs from your user directory (for example `~/.node-red`). If the proto file fails to load, open the protobuf-file config node and enter the absolute path on your machine.

name: protobuf wire bytes (hex)
